title: Processes, Signals, Jobs, and Resource Control · Command Retrieval
summary: Diagnose a worker that appears hung while preserving evidence, avoiding collateral termination, and distinguishing shell jobs from system processes. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.
Use ps, pgrep, pidof, top-like evidence, and proc data with filters that identify the intended process uniquely.
Send signals from least disruptive to forceful while accounting for handlers, permissions, descendants, and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Process Model · changed case
steps.instruction:
review case — Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2203; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.

Which action demonstrates Process Model without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.

content.text: Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2203; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Process Model condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Linux Kernel Userspace API Guide — Controls stable userspace interfaces and the boundary between command-line tools and kernel behavior.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A program file and a running process are the same object.

Inspect the Priority Limits trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2204; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A negative nice value means a process uses fewer resources.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Interpret nice values, scheduling priority, resource limits, and pressure evidence without equating priority with guaranteed resources. Artifact under review: a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Priority Limits, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Interpret nice values, scheduling priority, resource limits, and pressure evidence without equating priority with guaranteed resources.
